entries.definition:
humbled 4 subjugated, demoted (27:34) قَالَتْ إِنَّ الْمُلُوكَ إِذَا دَخَلُوا قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوهَا وَجَعَلُوا أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِهَا أَذِلَّةً she said, ‘Whenever kings/wealthy people go into a city, they corrupt it and make the notables of its inhabitants lowly.’
أَذَلُّ ʾadhall [elat., pl. أَذَلُّونَ ʾadhallūn] weaker, more/most debased, humiliated, humble, contemptible (63:8) لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الْأَذَلَّ the mighty will drive the contemptible out of it [the town].

ذَلُول dhalūl [quasi-act. part., pl. ذُلُل dhulul] 1 broken in, trained, docile, domesticated (2:71) إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ الْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِي الْحَرْثَ it is a cow, not broken to plough the earth or water the tillage/fields 2 accessible, stable, manageable (67:15) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا it is He who has made the earth accessible/manageable for you; so, travel its regions.
entries.definition_english:
humbled 4 subjugated, demoted (27:34) قَالَتْ إِنَّ الْمُلُوكَ إِذَا دَخَلُوا قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوهَا وَجَعَلُوا أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِهَا أَذِلَّةً she said, ‘Whenever kings/wealthy people go into a city, they corrupt it and make the notables of its inhabitants lowly.’
أَذَلُّ ʾadhall [elat., pl. أَذَلُّونَ ʾadhallūn] weaker, more/most debased, humiliated, humble, contemptible (63:8) لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الْأَذَلَّ the mighty will drive the contemptible out of it [the town].

ذَلُول dhalūl [quasi-act. part., pl. ذُلُل dhulul] 1 broken in, trained, docile, domesticated (2:71) إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ الْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِي الْحَرْثَ it is a cow, not broken to plough the earth or water the tillage/fields 2 accessible, stable, manageable (67:15) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا it is He who has made the earth accessible/manageable for you; so, travel its regions.

entries.definition:
dh–m–m pact, pledge, guarantee, protection; dirt, to revile, to abuse. Of this root, two forms occur five times in the Qur’an: ذِمَّة dhimmatun twice and مَذْمُوم madhmūm three times.
ذِمَّة dhimmatun [n.] a pledge of protection, a covenant of mutual protection, personal undertaking, good faith (9:10) لَا يَرْقُبُونَ فِي مُؤْمِنٍ إِلًّا وَلَا ذِمَّةً they respect, with regard to a believer, no tie of kinship or treaty.

مَذْمُوم madhmūm [pass. part.] discredited, blamed, reviled (17:22) لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَقْعُدَ مَذْمُومًا مَّخْذُولًا do not set besides God another deity, lest you end up reproved and forsaken.

مَذْمُوم madhmūm [pass. part.] discredited, blamed, reviled (17:22) لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَقْعُدَ مَذْمُومًا مَّخْذُولًا do not set besides God another deity, lest you end up reproved and forsaken.
